hero.headline: Uttaranchal University Online MBA in Logistics and Supply Chain Management: Syllabus, Fees and Career Outcomes
hero.sub: NAAC A+ | UGC-DEB Approved | 30% Early Bird Scholarship | Net fee from ₹94,000

sections.tldr: Uttaranchal University Online MBA in Logistics and Supply Chain Management covers International Logistics, Supply Chain Modelling Design and Simulation, Supply Chain Analytics, and Sustainability in Logistics and Supply Chain. One of the few budget-tier online MBAs with supply chain modelling and sustainability as standalone subjects. Net fee from ₹94,000 after 30% Early Bird. No-cost EMI ₹3,956/month. No bundled perks. NAAC A+.

about.body:
Uttaranchal University's Online MBA in Logistics and Supply Chain Management includes Supply Chain Modelling Design and Simulation and Sustainability in Logistics and Supply Chain as standalone subjects. At a net fee from ₹94,000, this is one of the most affordable online MBAs with dedicated supply chain modelling and sustainability content.

Semester 3 covers International Logistics (global freight, Incoterms, customs, cross-border trade) and Supply Chain Modelling Design and Simulation (network design, simulation techniques, scenario analysis for supply chain decisions). Semester 4 adds Supply Chain Analytics (demand sensing, inventory analytics, performance dashboards) and Sustainability in Logistics and Supply Chain (green logistics, carbon footprint measurement, circular economy, ESG reporting for supply chains).

The sustainability subject is forward-looking. With SEBI-mandated BRSR reporting and growing ESG compliance requirements, supply chain professionals who understand sustainability metrics are in demand.

Uttaranchal University, Dehradun holds NAAC A+ and UGC-DEB approval. The 30% Early Bird Scholarship reduces the fee from ₹1,40,000 to ₹98,000 (semester) or ₹94,000 (one-time). Exam fees included in quoted figures.

No bundled perks. No-cost EMI from ₹3,956/month directly through the university.

EdifyEdu verifies data independently and takes no commission from any university.

syllabus.note: Semesters 1-2 cover the common MBA core. Semesters 3-4 below show the Logistics and Supply Chain Management specialisation subjects.

subjects.name: Strategic Management
subjects.desc: Corporate strategy frameworks, competitive advantage, strategic planning, and business model analysis.

subjects.name: Supply Chain Management
subjects.desc: Supply chain design, demand planning, inventory management, supplier relationship management, and 3PL coordination.

subjects.name: Financial Statement Analysis
subjects.desc: Ratio analysis, DuPont decomposition, cash flow analysis, and financial health assessment.

subjects.name: International Logistics
subjects.desc: Global freight forwarding, Incoterms 2020, customs clearance procedures, cross-border trade documentation, containerisation, and free trade zone operations.

subjects.name: Supply Chain Modelling Design and Simulation
subjects.desc: Network design optimisation, simulation techniques for supply chain decisions, scenario analysis, demand variability modelling, and facility location planning.

subjects.name: Entrepreneurship Development
subjects.desc: Startup ideation, business plan development, and entrepreneurial risk management.

subjects.name: Corporate Governance
subjects.desc: Board structure, shareholder rights, corporate ethics, and governance frameworks.

subjects.name: Project Work
subjects.desc: Applied logistics/SCM project. Typically a supply chain optimisation study, logistics cost analysis, or sustainability audit.

subjects.name: Supply Chain Analytics
subjects.desc: Demand sensing, inventory analytics, supply chain KPI dashboards, predictive analytics for logistics, and performance benchmarking.

subjects.name: Sustainability in Logistics and Supply Chain
subjects.desc: Green logistics, carbon footprint measurement per unit shipped, reverse logistics, circular economy models, BRSR supply chain disclosure, and sustainable procurement.

whoHires.body:
Uttaranchal MBA Logistics and Supply Chain Management graduates target roles across the logistics value chain:

**Industries:** E-commerce fulfilment, 3PL/4PL providers, FMCG distribution, manufacturing supply chain, pharmaceutical logistics, cold chain, freight forwarding, and government logistics

**Entry-level roles:** Supply Chain Coordinator, Warehouse Executive, Logistics Executive, Procurement Officer, Distribution Executive, Transportation Planner

**Mid-level roles (3-5 years experience):** Supply Chain Manager, Logistics Manager, Procurement Manager, Warehouse Manager, Distribution Manager, S&OP Manager

**Senior roles (7+ years):** VP Supply Chain, Head of Logistics, Chief Supply Chain Officer, Director of Procurement, Head of Sustainability (supply chain)

**Employers:** E-commerce (Amazon, Flipkart fulfilment), 3PLs (Delhivery, DTDC, Blue Dart), FMCG (HUL, ITC, Nestle supply chain), auto (Tata Motors, Maruti), and North India regional logistics firms.

**Note:** UU provides career support. Dehradun location provides North India and Uttarakhand regional access.

skills.body:
The Logistics and SCM specialisation builds skills across supply chain design, analytics, and sustainability:

**Technical skills:**
- Supply Chain Modelling: network design optimisation, facility location analysis, demand variability simulation, and scenario planning
- International Logistics: Incoterms application, customs documentation, freight rate comparison (FCL vs LCL), and cross-border compliance
- Supply Chain Analytics: demand forecast accuracy tracking, inventory turnover analysis, OTIF measurement, and supply chain KPI dashboard design
- Sustainability: Scope 1/2/3 emissions accounting for logistics, carbon footprint per unit calculation, reverse logistics programme design, and BRSR disclosure

**Applied skills:**
- Procurement: vendor scorecard design, RFP process, supplier relationship management, and purchase order automation
- Warehouse: layout optimisation, WMS concepts, cycle count methodology, and warehouse KPI tracking

**Soft skills:** Managing multi-site logistics teams, negotiating freight rates with carriers, presenting supply chain performance to leadership, and coordinating cross-functional S&OP meetings.

comparisons.body:
Comparing Uttaranchal Online MBA Logistics and SCM against peer programmes:

**vs UPES Online MBA (logistics-adjacent):** UPES at approximately ₹1,95,000 with energy/logistics legacy brand and NIRF #45. UPES has established supply chain and logistics positioning. Uttaranchal at ₹94,000 is roughly half the cost with Supply Chain Modelling + Sustainability as standalone subjects. UPES wins on brand and logistics industry heritage. Uttaranchal wins on fee and sustainability curriculum depth.

**vs DPU Pune Online MBA Logistics and Supply Chain Management:** DPU at ₹1,89,400 with NIRF #41 and NAAC A++. DPU has green supply chain as a subject and stronger accreditation. Uttaranchal at ₹94,000 is half the cost with similar sustainability content plus supply chain modelling/simulation depth.

**vs Galgotias Online MBA Operations Management:** Galgotias at ₹86,400 covers Sourcing and Materials Management, SCM and Logistics, TQM. Galgotias is ₹7,600 cheaper but focused on operations, not logistics specifically. Uttaranchal has dedicated International Logistics + Supply Chain Modelling + Analytics + Sustainability.

**vs LPU Online MBA Logistics and Supply Chain Management:** LPU at approximately ₹1,61,000-₹2,00,000 with Coursera Plus bundled and zero-cost EMI. LPU has stronger brand and perks. Uttaranchal at ₹94,000 saves ₹67,000+ with supply chain modelling and sustainability depth.

fees.body:
The Uttaranchal University Online MBA in Logistics and Supply Chain Management has multiple payment tracks. All quoted figures include the 30% Early Bird Scholarship and examination fees.

**Total programme fee before scholarship:** ₹1,40,000
**After 30% Early Bird Scholarship:** ₹98,000 (semester track) / ₹94,000 (one-time track)
**Saving:** ₹42,000 for eligible applicants

**Payment options (net after Early Bird):**
- One-time payment: ₹94,000 (includes ₹4,000 additional concession)
- Annual payment: ₹47,000 x 2 years = ₹94,000 (includes ₹2,000/year additional concession)
- Semester payment: ₹24,500 x 4 semesters = ₹98,000 (standard Early Bird, no additional concession)

**Note:** One-time and annual tracks save ₹4,000 compared to semester track.

**No-cost EMI:** ₹3,956/month directly through the university.

**Exam fee:** ₹2,500/semester already included. No separate exam charges.

**Application/registration fee:** No separate application fee.

**Scholarships:** 30% Early Bird only. Confirm eligibility window with our counsellor.

**Bundled perks:** None.

Fees listed are indicative. EdifyEdu reconfirms fees with Uttaranchal University each quarter. Please reconfirm with our counsellor before any payment.

certificate.body:
Graduates receive an MBA degree certificate with Logistics and Supply Chain Management as the specialisation, issued by Uttaranchal University under UGC-DEB 2020 online mode standards.

The degree carries NAAC A+ accreditation and UGC-DEB approval. Valid for government logistics roles, PSU supply chain positions, railways/port authority management, and private-sector logistics employers. WES recognised. DigiLocker issuance is standard.

reviews.intro: Three reviews from Uttaranchal University Online MBA Logistics and SCM students. Ratings reflect individual experience.

items.name: Ajay K.
items.liked: Supply Chain Modelling and Simulation is a subject I haven't seen in any other sub-₹1L online MBA. The scenario analysis framework is directly applicable
items.disliked: Brand recognition is limited outside Uttarakhand. Most logistics employers in Mumbai or Bengaluru are unfamiliar with UU online MBA
items.body: I work in warehouse operations for an e-commerce company. The international logistics, supply chain modelling, and analytics subjects were directly applicable. At ₹94,000 after Early Bird, the value is strong. The sustainability subject covered BRSR reporting which my company is implementing. The trade-off is brand. UU is not in the same tier as LPU or DPU for national employer recognition.

items.name: Meena S.
items.liked: Sustainability in Logistics and Supply Chain is forward-looking. ESG and green logistics content is becoming mandatory knowledge
items.disliked: No perks, no Coursera, no certifications bundled. LPU at ₹1,61,000 includes Coursera Plus. The gap is noticeable
items.body: The curriculum covers both traditional logistics (international freight, customs) and modern themes (analytics, sustainability, modelling). The 30% Early Bird brought the fee down to a point where it was clearly affordable. Career services could be stronger for online students. Self-directed job search is the reality.

items.name: Vikram J.
items.liked: Exam fee included in semester fee is transparent. The ₹24,500/semester all-in pricing is straightforward
items.disliked: Early Bird has an admission window. If you miss it, the gross fee of ₹1,40,000 makes UU less competitive against CU or LPU
items.body: The programme delivers solid logistics and SCM content at a budget price. The supply chain analytics and modelling subjects gave me quantitative tools I did not have before. The LMS is functional but basic. Some recorded lectures need refreshing. The key is securing the Early Bird discount, without which the value proposition weakens.

faqs.question: Does the programme cover supply chain sustainability?
faqs.answer: Yes. Sustainability in Logistics and Supply Chain (Sem 4) covers green logistics, carbon footprint measurement, reverse logistics, circular economy, and BRSR supply chain disclosure. This is a standalone subject, not a module.

faqs.question: How does UU Logistics compare to UPES?
faqs.answer: UPES at approximately ₹1,95,000 has energy/logistics legacy brand and NIRF #45. UU at ₹94,000 is roughly half the cost with Supply Chain Modelling + Sustainability depth. UPES wins on brand. UU wins on fee.

faqs.question: Is the exam fee separate?
faqs.answer: No. ₹2,500/semester exam fee is included in the quoted ₹24,500 semester fee. No hidden charges.

faqs.question: What does the 30% Early Bird save?
faqs.answer: ₹42,000. Gross ₹1,40,000 drops to ₹98,000 (semester) or ₹94,000 (one-time). Available within the admission window. Confirm eligibility with our counsellor.

faqs.question: Are there bundled perks?
faqs.answer: No. No Coursera, LinkedIn Learning, or certifications are bundled. The programme is priced to reflect this no-frills positioning.

chooseIf: Supply Chain Modelling + Sustainability as standalone subjects at ₹94,000 net is unmatched at this price.
The 30% Early Bird makes this one of the most affordable logistics/SCM MBAs on the platform.
Exam fee included in semester fee (no hidden charges) matters to you.
You target logistics roles in North India/Uttarakhand where UU has regional recognition.
consider: National brand recognition matters. LPU, DPU, and UPES have stronger logistics/SCM positioning.
LPU at ₹1,61,000 includes Coursera Plus and placement support.
Without Early Bird, gross ₹1,40,000 is less competitive against peers with stronger brand.
DPU Pune at ₹1,89,400 with NAAC A++ and NIRF #41 offers stronger accreditation.
